data.content:
Here is the full breakdown of issue **#1 — "Navigation flicker when transitioning Detail → Detail on tablets"** (open, 2 comments, 0 labels).

---

### Source material

| Part | Content |
|------|---------|
| **Body** | A pinned meta-index notice — no technical bug description in the body itself. |
| **Comment 1** (SjJ1017) | Confirms the flicker on a Galaxy Tab S8 fold-to-tablet QA build; notes it has existed since **Compose BOM 2023-11** and is lower priority than an OOM crash in issue #14. |
| **Comment 2** (SjJ1017) | Network log shows **two** `getCharacter` requests fire on a second tap even though the first response has already returned — the ViewModel launches a new coroutine instead of de-duping. Suggests switching from `launchIn` to **`flatMapLatest`**. |

---

### Per-component breakdown

| Component | Summary |
|-----------|---------|
| **UI** | A visible flicker occurs on tablet-size screens (reported on Galaxy Tab S8 in fold-to-tablet mode) when navigating from one Detail screen to another Detail screen, indicating a Compose layout/rendering glitch during same-destination transitions. |
| **Navigation** | The detail-to-detail transition doesn't properly handle re-entry to the same destination type, causing the destination fragment/composable to briefly blank or redraw — suggesting the navigation graph or NavHost transition animation needs adjustment. |
| **Networking** | The ViewModel is launching a fresh coroutine (via `launchIn`) on each navigation rather than cancelling/replacing the previous one, which causes a duplicate `getCharacter` network call; the proposed fix is to use `flatMapLatest` to automatically cancel the prior request. |
| **Build / Gradle** | The regression has been present since **Compose BOM 2023-11**, pointing to a dependency-version-related change in the Compose navigation or animation libraries as the root trigger. |
| **Tests** | No test-related information (automated tests, test failures, or proposed test coverage) is mentioned in the issue body or either comment. |
